text.title: AI-Assisted Accessibility Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules grounding the ai-assisted-accessibility methodology plus the `skip-this-methodology` safety rule. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: The pipeline MUST use a Haiku-class model for scan + filter + rank AND a Sonnet-class model for fix generation. Using one model for both is forbidden.

rationale.source: Internal — model-routing policy + AI accessibility 2026 audits

rule.rationale: Haiku is cheap+fast for the mechanical filter; Sonnet is needed for the judgment in fix generation. Mixing them wastes cost (Sonnet on filtering) or quality (Haiku on fixes).

rule.statement: Every AI output (filtered issue list, fix proposal, alt text batch) MUST be reviewed by a human accessibility expert before any developer ticket is created. AI-direct-to-backlog is forbidden.

rationale.source: WCAG audit best practices + Internal — validation policy

rule.rationale: Without expert validation, AI false positives flood the backlog and erode developer trust in the whole pipeline.

rule.statement: Critical paths (auth, checkout, search) MUST be scanned by at least two tools (axe + pa11y) and the union of violations passed to the AI filter.
rule.rationale: Single-tool scans miss tool-specific blind spots; cross-checking critical paths catches violations only one tool sees.

rule.statement: Every AI-generated fix MUST cite the WCAG success criterion (e.g., `2.4.4 Link Purpose`) and the file:line it patches. Unanchored fixes are rejected.
rule.rationale: Fixes without WCAG citation drift from spec; without file:line they cannot be merged.

rule.statement: Bulk alt-text generation MUST be throttled to N images per batch (typical N=50) with an expert sample review of ≥ 10% of each batch.
rule.rationale: Unlimited bulk + zero review produces drift in tone + accuracy; throttling + sampling is the defensible middle.

rule.statement: Skip this methodology when the preconditions in `## Applies If` are not all true, or any item in `## Skip If` holds. The decision tree routes to this rule on those branches.
rule.rationale: A methodology that runs on the wrong input wastes tokens and produces misleading artifacts. The explicit skip rule is the safety valve.
